global.title: Deadly shooting at San Diego Islamic center - NBC News
global.summary: Police say two teenage gunmen opened fire at a San Diego Islamic center, killing three people, including a security guard, before taking their own lives.
global.whyItMatters: This is a sobering reminder of rising violence against faith communities and the need to pray, teach peace, and strengthen care for vulnerable congregations.

global.title: The Latest: World shares and oil prices react to Trump’s warning to Tehran over stalled negotiations - Greenwich Time
global.summary: Markets fell and oil prices rose after renewed U.S.-Iran tensions, with a drone strike near the UAE’s nuclear plant underscoring fears of wider conflict in the region.
global.whyItMatters: Escalation in the Middle East affects global stability, economic pressure, and prayerful attention to peace, especially for churches with ties to the region.

global.title: A dust storm strikes Iraq's Najaf, reducing visibility - NBC News
global.summary: A severe dust storm swept through Najaf, sharply reducing visibility and highlighting ongoing environmental strain in Iraq.
global.whyItMatters: Climate and environmental disruptions increasingly shape human vulnerability, displacement, and ministry needs around the world.

local.title: Montana nonprofit releases interactive map of housing affordability in all 56 counties - Bozeman Daily Chronicle
local.summary: A Montana nonprofit has released an interactive county-by-county housing affordability map aimed at helping residents and leaders better understand local housing pressures.
local.whyItMatters: Housing strain is a pastoral issue too, affecting families, generosity, stability, and how churches think about mercy, advocacy, and community life.

local.title: “The Nazi and The Rabbi” discussion breaks down barriers - Bozeman Daily Chronicle
local.summary: A public conversation highlighted unlikely relationship-building and the hard work of breaking down hatred and social barriers.
local.whyItMatters: This offers a timely picture of reconciliation, repentance, and bridge-building that can inform preaching and local peacemaking ministry.

persecutedChurch.title: Open Doors: Number of Christians persecuted worldwide rises to 388 million
persecutedChurch.summary: A new Open Doors report says the number of Christians facing persecution or serious risk worldwide has risen to 388 million.
persecutedChurch.whyItMatters: This is a direct call for informed prayer, global awareness, and solidarity with suffering believers.

persecutedChurch.title: ADF Kills 43 Christians on Maundy Thursday - International Christian Concern
persecutedChurch.summary: International Christian Concern reports that 43 Christians were killed in an ADF attack in eastern Democratic Republic of Congo on Maundy Thursday.
persecutedChurch.whyItMatters: It is a painful reminder that Holy Week violence against believers remains a present reality and deserves prayerful attention from the church.

leadership.title: Why Today’s Best Leaders Ask, "What Do You Need Right Now?" - Forbes
leadership.summary: A Forbes piece argues that attuned leadership begins with asking people what they need and then responding with consistent follow-through that builds trust and psychological safety.
leadership.whyItMatters: For a pastor, this is a practical leadership cue for staff care, volunteer health, and shepherding people with presence rather than assumption.

goodNews.title: Homeless Seattle man gets new lease on life after dumpster dive discovery - New York Post
goodNews.summary: After helping recover something meaningful for a grieving family, a Seattle man who had lived on the streets for seven years has now moved into his first apartment.
goodNews.whyItMatters: This is a hopeful picture of dignity, kindness, and how one act of compassion can open the door to restoration.
